channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Regional Mediators Rush to Save U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
item.description: Pakistan and Qatar have dispatched teams to Iran under the looming threat of resumed war, after weeks of diplomacy failed to produce an agreement.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Elian Peltier and Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: People walking through an intersection in Tehran beneath a billboard showing the former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, earlier this month. He was killed in February, and succeeded by his son.

item.title: Stocks and Oil Prices Tick Up Amid Impasse Over Reopening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Nearly three months since the fighting began, disagreements remain over the fate of Iran’s uranium stockpile and transit fees for the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran and Oman in Talks Over Strait of Hormuz Ship Payment System
item.description: The discussions suggest that the United States and the Iranian government may not be close to reaching a deal to end a war that has badly damaged the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, Vivian Nereim, Erika Solomon and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: GOP Pulls Measure on Trump’s War Powers in Iran, Lacking Votes to Defeat It
item.description: House Republican leaders abruptly scrapped a planned vote on a measure to direct President Trump to end the conflict or win authorization for it, amid party defections and absences.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro, Robert Jimison and Michael Gold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: In a Rarity, Republicans Stand Up to Trump
item.description: President Trump faced a wall of opposition from Senate G.O.P. lawmakers, in part over his plan to create a $1.8 billion fund to reward his allies.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Senator Susan Collins, Republican of Maine, is known to oppose the most extreme parts of President Trump’s agenda.

item.title: Fact-Checking the Trump Administration’s Inaccurate Claims About the $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: The president, vice president and acting attorney general have offered a series of inaccurate claims to defend an unusual fund announced this week.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu and Jamie Leventhal
item.media:credit: Carolyn Van Houten for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has suggested the $1.8 billion fund is not particularly unusual, but critics say it essentially establishes a way to funnel taxpayer money to Mr. Trump’s allies.

item.title: Disconnect Widens Between Mamdani and Pro-Israel Jewish Leaders
item.description: In recent days, Mayor Zohran Mamdani has taken actions that have made the relationship between his office and some Jewish New York leaders more fraught.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Sally Goldenberg
item.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
item.media:description: An annual parade on Fifth Avenue in Manhattan celebrating Israel typically attracts the mayor of New York City, but Zohran Mamdani has said he plans to skip the event this year.

item.title: ‘Colbert’ Is Done. ‘SNL’ is Stale. Where Does Late-Night TV Go From Here? | Cannonball
item.description: Maybe “Saturday Night Live” should go instead.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Morris and Jason Zinoman
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by The New York Times; Photo by Caro Scarimbolo/NBC via Getty Images
item.media:description: Mikey Day, Marcello Hernández and Colin Jost on the “Weekend Update” segment of “Saturday Night Live.”

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’: Laughing Well Is the Best Revenge
item.description: The “Late Show” cancellation was a disappointment. But a surreally lovely final episode turned it into a cancellebration.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert and an array of famous friends gave “The Late Show” a joyous send-off on Thursday night.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Last ‘Late Show’ Features Famous Friends and Paul McCartney
item.description: Colbert was joined by famous friends, fellow hosts and special guests like Paul McCartney for the finale of “The Late Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
item.media:description: Stephen Colbert’s final “Late Show” included celebrity guests, musical performances and an absurdist final bit.

item.title: Warsh Takes Charge of Fed Facing Rising Inflation Threat
item.description: The economic backdrop that Kevin M. Warsh inherits as chair of the Federal Reserve does not call for the interest rate cuts that President Trump wants.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh was sworn in as Federal Reserve chair during a ceremony with President Trump on Friday at the White House.

item.title: An Option That Can Help Cut Housing Prices Catches On
item.description: Preapproved plans trim weeks or months off the process of getting a building permit. “Every month of delay adds costs that ultimately land on the buyer,” one housing expert said.
item.dc:creator: Ann Carrns

item.title: Biden Won’t Endorse Becerra, or Anyone Else, in California Governor Race
item.description: Former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. is not planning to endorse his health and human services secretary, Xavier Becerra, or any other candidates in the race.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Godofredo A. Vásquez
item.media:description: Xavier Becerra, a Democratic candidate for California governor, served as health and human services secretary under President Joseph R. Biden Jr.

item.title: Wasserman Schultz Will Run in Majority-Black District in South Florida
item.description: Republicans drew the former D.N.C. chairwoman out of her district. Her decision to run in Florida’s 20th Congressional District, however, has stoked tensions with Black Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:description: Representative Debbie Wasserman Schultz, a Democrat, will run in a majority-Black district in South Florida after Republicans drew her out of her own district.

item.title: Lutnick Donated $5 Million to House Republicans Before Epstein Testimony
item.description: Howard Lutnick, the commerce secretary, made the donation after agreeing to an interview with lawmakers about his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Ana Swanson
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Howard Lutnick, the secretary of commerce, at a hearing in Washington last month.

item.title: Poll Suggests a Possible Path Forward for Democrats
item.description: The divisions are clear, but there’s more common ground than one might expect within the coalition, a Times/Siena poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn
item.media:credit: Illustration by Akshita Chandra/The New York Times; Photograph by Ryasick, via Getty Images

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil to Appeal to Supreme Court in Effort to Halt Deportation
item.description: The move was expected to come after a federal appeals court on Friday ruled against Mr. Khalil, who became the face of President Trump’s crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:credit: Scott Heins for The New York Times
item.media:description: Khalil Mahmoud will likely ask the Supreme Court to review the decision to deport him after a lower court declined to hear his case.

item.title: Mac Barnett Faces Blowback for His Criticism of Children’s Books
item.description: Mac Barnett, the national ambassador for young people’s literature published a manifesto aimed at adults. Then came the blowback.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Egan
item.category: Make Believe: On Telling Stories to Children (Book)
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fellow children’s book authors took issue with Mac Barnett for points he made in his new book, “Make Believe.”

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson Wed in West Palm Beach
item.description: President Trump’s eldest son was married in Palm Beach, Fla. The president described celebrations this weekend as a “small, little private affair.”
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan and Tammy LaGorce
item.media:description: Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson were legally married on Thursday in West Palm Beach, Fla., according to Palm Beach County marriage records.

item.title: The Civil Rights Era Is Collapsing Before Our Eyes
item.description: America is witnessing the greatest decimation of Black political power in over a century.
item.dc:creator: Nikole Hannah-Jones

item.title: On the Ground in South Sudan: Why Akobo Faces an Ebola Risk
item.description: Hunger and conflict are on the minds of the residents of Akobo, where an outbreak could have devastating consequences.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Ed Ram
item.media:credit: Ed Ram for The New York Times

item.title: American Doctor With Ebola Says He Is ‘Optimistic’ for Recovery
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford, a medical missionary in the Democratic Republic of Congo, tested positive for Ebola on Sunday. He is receiving treatment in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Dr. Peter Stafford being transported to Germany for medical treatment for Ebola.

item.title: Ebola Outbreak: What to Know About Bundibugyo Virus Symptoms and Spread
item.description: Here’s what to know about the symptoms and spread of the virus, which has sickened people in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda.
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum

item.title: Memorial Day Travel Forecast: Gas Prices, Traffic and Flights
item.description: Most U.S. travelers will be driving to their destinations for the long weekend, kicking off what is expected to be a busy summer travel season.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:description: A record 45 million Americans are expected to hit the highways over the long weekend, according to AAA.

item.title: With Gas Prices Through the Roof, Will Renting an EV or Hybrid Car Save You Money?
item.description: With gas prices through the roof, more travelers are considering alternative vehicles to control costs. But how much will you really save?
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac
item.media:credit: Drew Kelly for The New York Times
item.media:description: The great American road trip is at risk this year because of rising fuel prices. Some travelers are looking to rent hybrid or electric vehicles to save money.

item.title: Watch Scorsese Make a Mean Sandwich in ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’
item.description: The director Jon Favreau narrates a sequence featuring a character who works at a food truck and is voiced by Martin Scorsese.
item.dc:creator: Mekado Murphy
item.category: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu (Movie)

item.title: ‘Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu’: What to Know Before Seeing the Movie
item.description: It draws from the Disney+ series. Here’s a reminder of the events of the show and how those fit into the larger franchise.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.category: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu (Movie)
item.media:description: Pedro Pascal as the Mandalorian and his little friend in a scene from the new movie.

item.title: A.I. Is a Job Creator
item.description: A.I. will create more jobs than it will kill.
item.dc:creator: David M. Solomon

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Finale Didn’t Mean What It Seemed To
item.description: You’re going to miss corporate media.
item.dc:creator: Michael Hirschorn

item.title: We Want to Hear From Delivery Workers in New York City.
item.description: The New York Times is examining the working conditions of delivery app workers.
item.dc:creator: Dionne Searcey and Bianca Pallaro
item.media:description: A delivery worker rides on a city street.

item.title: Google Appeals Landmark Ruling Declaring It a Monopolist in Search
item.description: Google asked a federal appeals court on Friday to reverse a judge’s ruling that it had violated antitrust laws, as well as a requirement it share some data with rivals.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Google campus in Mountain View, Calif.

item.title: SpaceX to Attempt Launch of Updated Starship Rocket
item.description: If a problem with the launch tower is fixed, SpaceX will try to launch on Friday evening.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang
item.media:description: Sunrise at Starbase in South Texas on Friday, as the SpaceX Starship rocket was preparing for another launch attempt.

item.title: New Taliban Decree on Divorce Formalizes Child Marriage in Afghanistan, U.N. Warns
item.description: A new Afghan law requires girls to wait until puberty before seeking to get out of a marriage. It also requires mediation for women seeking to escape an abusive husband.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pharmacy in Kabul, Afghanistan, on Tuesday. The Taliban government has imposed some of the world’s toughest restrictions on women and girls.
